@@A. The trace exhibits several issues, including disobeying task specifications (agents repeating instructions instead of solving the problem), task derailment (agents proposing simplified implementations instead of finding the actual code), information withholding (agents not sharing crucial information), weak verification (reviewers failing to identify critical bugs), step repetition (repeatedly asking to continue without progress), and premature termination (stopping the task due to an invalid token). There are also instances of ignoring other agents' suggestions and action-reasoning mismatches. The most prominent issue is the agents' inability to effectively collaborate and build upon each other's work, leading to stalled progress and incorrect solutions.
